response.justification: The candidate is a strong polyglot engineer with genuine systems-programming depth (low-latency DLang services, async I/O, event-driven architectures) and an admirable testing culture, but his hands-on C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months in 2014–2015 plus a thesis project, and C++ does not even appear in his skills list. The role demands a current, expert-level C++/STL developer with industrial automation exposure, IEC 62443 SDL practice, and MQTT/OT protocol familiarity — none of which are evidenced. His recent trajectory is web/TypeScript product engineering, making this a poor fit despite his seniority.
strengths: Real low-latency, concurrent, networked systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at high request volumes
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and a track record of mentoring via workshops and courses
Broad full-stack and event-driven architecture experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded contexts
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014–2015); C++ is absent from his stated skills
No industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL, MQTT, OPC UA, or OT-protocol exposure anywhere in the history
Recent five-plus years are web/TypeScript product work, far from high-performance industrial C++ platform development

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or automation domain background
Transferable concurrent/networked systems and mentoring skills
